rule_name: IAM Access Key Created for Another User
description: Detects when an IAM user creates an access key for a different user. This is a common persistence technique where an attacker with admin access creates new credentials for an account they control or want to maintain access to, without needing to know the original password.
triage_steps: Compare userIdentity.arn (who made the call) with requestParameters.userName (who the key is for)
If they differ, this is a cross-user key creation — escalate immediately
Check if the target user is a service account or human account
Review the source IP and user agent for anomalies
Check for subsequent API calls using the newly created access key
false_positive_notes: Legitimate scenarios include: automated user onboarding pipelines, IT admins provisioning new employees, CI/CD systems creating service account keys. Validate against change management records.
tuning_guidance: To reduce noise, add a filter excluding known automation roles or service accounts that routinely create keys. Consider correlating with a whitelist of approved key-creation principals.
response_actions: Disable the newly created access key immediately
Investigate the principal who created the key — check for signs of compromise
Review all recent actions by both the creator and the target user
Rotate credentials for the target user if compromise is confirmed
Check CloudTrail for any API calls made using the new key
